questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does a regular pentagon have?
questions.explanation: A regular polygon with 'n' sides has exactly 'n' lines of symmetry.

questions.text: Which of the following letters has vertical line symmetry?
questions.explanation: The letter M can be divided into two matching halves by a vertical line.

questions.text: An equilateral triangle has rotational symmetry of order:
questions.explanation: It looks the same at 120°, 240°, and 360° rotations.

questions.text: The angle of rotation for a square is:
questions.explanation: 360° divided by the order of rotational symmetry (4) is 90°.

questions.text: Which figure has only one line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: An isosceles triangle has only one line of symmetry passing through the vertex between the equal sides.

questions.text: A circle has ________ lines of symmetry.
questions.explanation: Any line passing through the center of a circle is a line of symmetry.

questions.text: The letter 'S' has which type of symmetry?
questions.explanation: The letter S has rotational symmetry of order 2 but no line of symmetry.

questions.text: What is the order of rotational symmetry of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: A rectangle looks the same after a 180° and 360° rotation.

questions.text: Which of these polygons has no line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A scalene triangle has all sides and angles different, so it cannot be folded into matching halves.

questions.text: Order of rotational symmetry for a regular hexagon is:
questions.explanation: A regular hexagon fits onto itself 6 times during a full 360° turn.
